title: Transportation in Seattle - Visit Seattle
content: ## Seattle is easy to navigate with a wide variety of smart transportation options. Green icon of a person in a wheelchair, symbolizing accessibility or facilities for individuals with disabilities, on a white background. Sound Transit’s Link light rail 1 Line makes trips from Angle Lake Station to Lynwood through downtown Seattle making 21 stops along the way, including downtown Seattle and SEA Airport. Trains arrive every 8 to 15 minutes, depending on the time of day, and take about 40 minutes to travel between Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (SEA) and Westlake Station in downtown Seattle. A Seattle Metro bus travels down a city street, with cyclists and cars nearby. A red streetcar is stopped at a station on a city street. This line consists of 10 stops and stretches 2.5 miles, connecting to Link light rail at the Capitol Hill and International District stations, the Sounder Commuter Rail and Amtrak intercity rail at the King Street Station, and Metro Transit buses at several points.
